claims.claim: Vapi: VAD runs in separate process, audio via stdin/stdout pipes, ASCII probability scores, auto-respawn without dropping conversation (vapi-pipeline-2 line 16)
claims.found: 'VAD runs in a separate process. Audio flows between processes via stdin/stdout pipes, probability scores returned as ASCII strings. When the process fails, the system automatically respawns it without dropping the conversation.' — exact match at the Reliability paragraph

claims.claim: Pipecat's analogous primitive is its IPC layer at src/pipecat/ipc/proc_pool.py
claims.found: No ipc/ directory exists anywhere in pipecat; no proc_pool.py and no multiprocessing/process-pool code in src/pipecat. The path src/pipecat/ipc/proc_pool.py does not exist. proc_pool.py only exists in LiveKit at agents/livekit-agents/livekit/agents/ipc/proc_pool.py — the note copied LiveKit's path onto pipecat.
claims.correction: Remove the pipecat IPC/proc_pool claim. Pipecat has no IPC process-pool layer. The only proc_pool.py in the clones is LiveKit's: _clones/agents/livekit-agents/livekit/agents/ipc/proc_pool.py

claims.claim: Pipecat runs VAD as an in-process FrameProcessor at src/pipecat/audio/vad/, not a separate OS process
claims.found: src/pipecat/audio/vad/ contains VADAnalyzer(ABC) (vad_analyzer.py:62) plus silero/krisp/aic analyzers — these are analyzer classes, not a FrameProcessor. The actual FrameProcessor wrapping VAD is at src/pipecat/processors/audio/vad_processor.py. The core mechanism claim (VAD runs in-process, not a separate OS process) is correct.
claims.correction: The FrameProcessor lives at pipecat/src/pipecat/processors/audio/vad_processor.py; the cited audio/vad/ dir holds the analyzer ABC (vad_analyzer.py). Mechanism (in-process) is accurate.

claims.claim: LiveKit runs jobs in a process pool at ipc/proc_pool.py, at job granularity not per-component
claims.found: _clones/agents/livekit-agents/livekit/agents/ipc/proc_pool.py exists, alongside supervised_proc.py, job_proc_executor.py, proc_client.py — confirming job-granularity process pooling with supervision.

claims.claim: Mechanism: separate VAD process buys per-component crash isolation + GIL relief, paying IPC/stdin-stdout serialization cost
claims.cited: note Detail/Prior-art divergence (synthesis from vapi quote)
claims.found: Reasonable synthesis directly grounded in the vapi source's separate-process + stdin/stdout + respawn statement; GIL/IPC-cost framing is interpretive but standard and not contradicted by source.

summary: Vapi source quote (line 16) is verbatim-accurate. LiveKit proc_pool path SUPPORTED. The note's central code claim is WRONG: it attributes an IPC layer 'src/pipecat/ipc/proc_pool.py' to Pipecat, but Pipecat has no ipc/ directory and no proc_pool.py at all — that path is LiveKit's, copied onto Pipecat. The Pipecat VAD FrameProcessor is MISCITED (it's processors/audio/vad_processor.py, not the audio/vad/ analyzer dir), though the in-process mechanism claim holds. overall=FAIL due to the fabricated Pipecat IPC path.
